name: French Fantan Rolls
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the scalded milk, butter, sugar, and salt in a small saucepan.
Stir until the butter melts. Cool mixture to 105-115 degrees using a thermometer.
2. Dissolve the yeast in 1/4 cup warm water in a large mixing bowl. Stir in the milk mixture, eggs, butter extract, and lemon extract (if used). Gradually stir in enough flour to make a soft dough. Start with 5-1/2 cups of flour and add more until the dough is no longer sticky and is soft and silky.
3. Turn the dough out onto a lightly floured surface; knead until smooth and elastic, about 5 minutes. Alternatively, let your mixer do the work.
4. Place the dough into a large greased bowl, turning to coat the top with oil. Cover and let rise in a warm place until doubled in bulk, about 1 hour.
5. Punch down the dough to release air bubbles. Form the rolls into desired shapes (fan-shaped or clover-shaped). For fan-shaped rolls: make small lemon-sized balls; for clover-shaped rolls: make three walnut-sized balls per roll.
6. Place each ball of dough into large greased muffin cups. Optionally, roll the balls in melted butter before placing them in the pans.
7. Cover and let rise in a warm place for about 30 minutes or until doubled in size.
8. Preheat the oven to 425 degrees.
9. Bake for 10-12 minutes or until golden brown.
10. After the first rising, punch down the dough and divide it into two halves. Roll each half into a 12 x 6-inch rectangle. Brush with butter and cut into six 1-inch strips. Stack the strips (butter-side up) and cut into 12 pieces. Place the stacks into well-buttered large muffin cups (sideways).
11. Follow the rising directions again.
